predictions.text: War-risk insurance for tankers transiting the Strait of Hormuz will be quoted at or above 2.0% of hull value for at least one voyage.
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolves YES if Reuters, Lloyd’s List, The Times (UK)/Splash247, or a major marine insurance broker (e.g., Marsh, Aon, Willis) reports war-risk premium/additional premium for a Hormuz transit quoted >=2.0% of hull value (or explicitly equivalent) for tanker shipping at least once by the deadline.
evidence: U.S. announced a $20B oil-tanker reinsurance program, indicating elevated and policy-relevant maritime risk pricing.
Ongoing U.S.–Israel strikes on Iran raise the probability of shipping disruptions and insurer repricing.
monitoring_signals: Reuters/Lloyd’s List reports on Gulf war-risk additional premiums
Joint War Committee market commentary and broker client notes (Marsh/Aon/Willis)
Announcements of attacks, seizures, or missile/drone incidents near Hormuz
predictions.rationale: Sustained conflict can rapidly reprice marine war risk; a 2% quote is high but achievable during acute escalation.

predictions.text: The U.S. government will increase the announced oil-tanker reinsurance backstop from $20B to at least $30B (ceiling/authorization).
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolves YES if an official U.S. government release/document (White House, Treasury, DOT/MARAD, FEMA, or Federal Register notice) states the reinsurance facility/backstop ceiling/authorization is increased to >=$30B by the deadline.
evidence: CNBC reports a $20B tanker reinsurance program was announced, implying active policy management of maritime risk.
monitoring_signals: Treasury/DOT/MARAD press releases and Federal Register notices
Statements from major shippers/insurers about capacity shortfalls
Congressional letters/hearings referencing tanker insurance capacity
predictions.rationale: If premiums spike or coverage gaps persist, policymakers may scale the backstop to stabilize flows.

predictions.text: Iran’s nationwide internet disruption will substantially ease, with connectivity returning to at least 70% of baseline for 48 consecutive hours.
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolves YES if NetBlocks (or a similarly cited major internet observatory used by Reuters/AP/major outlets) reports Iran’s national connectivity reaches >=70% of pre-blackout baseline and remains at or above that level for at least 48 consecutive hours by the deadline.
evidence: Iran’s internet blackout has extended into a second week, per NetBlocks reporting cited by CNBC.
monitoring_signals: NetBlocks time series and incident updates
Cloudflare Radar country-level connectivity signals
Reports of easing curfews/security measures or restoration of mobile networks
predictions.rationale: Extended blackouts are costly and difficult to sustain; partial restoration is plausible even if controls remain.

predictions.text: A bill to prohibit members of Congress and/or senior federal officials from profiting from prediction markets will receive a recorded committee vote in either chamber.
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolves YES if an official committee record shows a recorded vote (roll-call or recorded voice vote) on reporting/advancing a bill that bans or materially restricts members of Congress and/or senior executive-branch officials from trading/profiting on prediction markets (including event contracts) by the deadline.
evidence: Senators publicly launched an effort to bar federal officials from profiting via prediction markets.
monitoring_signals: Senate/House committee calendars (Rules, Homeland Security & Governmental Affairs, Ethics, Administration)
Bill text/companion introductions and co-sponsor growth
Media coverage of prediction-market controversies involving public officials
predictions.rationale: There is visible sponsor attention, but floor time is scarce; committee action is the more likely near-term milestone.

predictions.text: The CFTC will publish formal proposed guidance or a notice of proposed rulemaking addressing the permissibility of political/war-related event contracts.
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolves YES if the CFTC posts on its website or in the Federal Register either (a) a notice of proposed rulemaking, (b) interpretive guidance, or (c) a policy statement explicitly addressing political event contracts and/or contracts tied to war/assassination/armed conflict (as a class) by the deadline.
evidence: Growing political attention to prediction markets increases pressure for clearer federal derivatives policy.
monitoring_signals: CFTC meeting agendas and open commission votes
Federal Register CFTC notices referencing event contracts
Public comment requests or staff advisories on contract certification
predictions.rationale: Higher market visibility and political scrutiny raise the odds of CFTC clarification beyond case-by-case enforcement.

predictions.text: OFAC will designate at least 10 Iran-linked individuals and/or entities for cyber activity targeting U.S. critical infrastructure.
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolves YES if the U.S. Treasury (OFAC) announces sanctions adding >=10 individuals/entities in a single action (or same-day package) where the press release/fact sheet explicitly cites Iran-linked cyber activity targeting U.S. critical infrastructure (energy, water, transportation, financial, healthcare) by the deadline.
evidence: Ongoing Iran conflict environment elevates the likelihood of cyber retaliation and U.S. attribution/sanctions responses.
monitoring_signals: Treasury/OFAC press releases and SDN list updates
CISA/FBI attribution statements that often precede sanctions
Major incident reporting affecting U.S. infrastructure operators
predictions.rationale: Sanctions are a common, fast-turn policy response if cyber activity is attributed amid heightened conflict.

predictions.text: The U.S. will publicly confirm deployment of an additional THAAD battery or at least two Patriot batteries to the Gulf region tied to Iran escalation.
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolves YES if DoD/CENTCOM, or a major outlet citing DoD officials, reports deployment/positioning in the Gulf region of either (a) one additional THAAD battery or (b) >=2 Patriot batteries, explicitly linked to Iran escalation/force protection, by the deadline.
evidence: Conflict has entered a second week with elevated regional spillover risk, increasing force-protection measures.
monitoring_signals: DoD/CENTCOM press briefings and posture statements
Airlift tracking and host-nation announcements
Reports of ballistic missile/drone threats to bases or partners
predictions.rationale: Air defense deployments are a standard and visible escalation-control tool during regional conflict.

predictions.text: ICE Brent front-month will experience a 30-day realized volatility of at least 55% annualized.
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolves YES if, using daily settlement prices for ICE Brent front-month, the annualized standard deviation of daily log returns over any rolling 30-trading-day window reaches or exceeds 55% by the deadline (as calculated by a major data provider or reproducibly from public settlement data).
evidence: Second-week continuation of Iran conflict increases tail-risk repricing in oil and shipping markets.
monitoring_signals: Daily Brent settlement moves and implied volatility (options)
News on Hormuz security incidents and export disruptions
IEA/OPEC+ emergency communications
predictions.rationale: Even without sustained price spikes, conflict-driven uncertainty can push realized volatility above crisis-like thresholds.

predictions.text: OpenAI will publish an updated public policy document that explicitly permits some defense/national-security use cases while adding new constraints on weaponization.
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolves YES if OpenAI publishes a dated update to its publicly accessible policies (e.g., Usage Policies, Safety/Preparedness, or similar) that (a) explicitly references defense, military, national security, or government military agencies as permitted in some form, and (b) also adds or clarifies prohibitions/constraints related to weaponization/targeting, by the deadline.
evidence: Reported internal friction around a Pentagon deal suggests pressure to clarify public-facing defense-use boundaries.
monitoring_signals: OpenAI policy changelogs and blog posts
DoD/OpenAI public contracting announcements and FAQs
Additional high-profile staff departures tied to defense work
predictions.rationale: Public scrutiny plus internal dissent often leads to policy clarifications that balance commercialization with safety constraints.

predictions.text: China will publicly confirm at least one successful recovery (landing or splashdown retrieval) of an orbital-class reusable rocket first stage.
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolves YES if Chinese state media (e.g., Xinhua/CCTV) or an official Chinese aerospace entity announces and provides evidence of recovery of an orbital-capable launch vehicle first stage (landing on pad/ship or recovered from sea) after a launch by the deadline.
evidence: Reports highlight China accelerating investments in rockets amid U.S.–China space-power competition.
monitoring_signals: Chinese launch manifests and test campaign cadence
State media coverage of reusability milestones
NOTAMs and maritime hazard zones consistent with recovery operations
predictions.rationale: Strategic focus on launch capacity and cost could accelerate a first publicly acknowledged orbital-class recovery, but technical and disclosure uncertainty remain high.
